unreleased.label: What works in co-op right now
unreleased.rangeNote: Current build 0.4.8 — very early access, growing every week.

bullets: The whole opening plays together: the drive in, the hiker at the window, cooking, and the Jenga + Ouija mini-games all stay in sync.
Mike and every cabin character — the hiker, Nora, the cat — move and act the same on both screens.
Now covers the dark return-to-cabin run, not just the first visit.
Mike drives in and the whole order plays out shared — the booth, the pizza, the phone, the truck.
The cashier, the hobo, the hiker and the box-folding worker are all synced.
The full highway drive is shared — the radio, the deer, the school bus and the final conversation hit both players at the same time.
Desks, the computers and their on-screen windows, the coffee machine, vending, fridge and bathrooms all stay in sync.
The office workers and the janitor act the same for both players.
The phone call, the elevator and the parking-lot beats are shared.
The stranger, Mike and the walking cop are synced.
You see each other as real in-game characters with name tags, not floating capsules.
The host now waits for the second player during loading screens, so you stay together through every scene change.
Built-in F11 menu to host or join — no config files to touch.
Office and Parking Lot added; deeper Cabin, Pizzeria and Road Trip coverage; in-game host/join menu; real player avatars with name tags.
Full join handshake so the second player loads straight into the host's exact state; Cabin characters and mini-games synced.
First working co-op: host and client connect, scenes sync, world / doors / dialogue mirror between both players.
Initial build: host and spectator, scene and camera sync.
